item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this SCP, I have theories about what it means, but it's just classic creepiness aswell +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this post mainly because Ronald Reagan became president of the United States 1981.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can see in the earlier discussions that the author meant absolutely none of this except the title references.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My fellow Americans,

Moving speech
Close

As the 1776th upvoter on such a patriotic article, I have some words for the public. First, I definitely traded weapons for hostages. Second, I definitely funded the Taliban. Third, the only good thing that came out of my presidency was this bomb music video. Also this one.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: All the images used in this article are edited versions of a recording of the "Evil Empire" speech, which can be found here on Wikimedia Commons and is in the Public Domain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does it work when reproduced on a DVD or any other format like a mp4?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfection.

item.title: Love it!
item.content:encoded: This was actually the entry that got me into SCP. I had never even heard of SCP before stumbling upon this entry and oh boy, am I glad I found this. I keep coming back to this one because it's just so unique and intriguing. Thank you for getting me into this fandom and for creating such a terrifying story to enjoy on repeat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I first read this in 2013 or so, I barely knew who Ronald Reagan was, and my reaction to this article was horror. But revisiting this article, having grown up and learned about what Reagan was, it makes a lot more sense. It's a monument to all the evil and violence he represented, and all that that would come after him. The hooded figure of Death cuts him up in effigy, and he is forced to stand and recite twisted parodies of the speeches he gave in life, more honest versions, with all the banality stripped away and the cruelty shown up front. That's where the real horror sets in, but there's also a sense of catharsis, for me at least.

I think the reference in the title to Godspeed You! Black Emperor is the best evidence that my interpretation is correct, but I don't think the author is around anymore, so we'll never know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: even without outside knowledge of the references, this one has always really disturbed me, particularly that last addendum. an easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is probably my favorite SCP of all time, and it’s the one that got me really invested in wiki. It shows that an SCP doesn’t have to be life-threatening to be scary, it can just feel wrong. The juxtaposition of well-known speeches with new horrifying yet incomprehensible additions perfectly creates that feeling. This still makes me shiver a bit every time I read it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can I get some context please?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I…… Just if I found this I would go absolutely insane

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is, like, the SCP that everyone knows about but no one talks about.

item.title: Im upvoter 1500!
item.content:encoded: I love this one. I love all the images and tests, and making jokes about it too. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is a predominant question: Why am I here? What can I do to make it better? How can I do what is right?It is a predominant question: Why am I here? What can I do to make it better? How can I do what is right?It is a predominant question: Why am I here? What can I do to make it better? How can I do what is right?

item.title: SqueakyT
item.content:encoded:
This was disturbingly enjoyable. I liked this way too much. I replaced Former President Reagan with our current President and I'm not sure I should have just said that…

brb There's a loud knock at my door……

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to sleep so good tonight….
+1

item.title: Re: Explanation
item.content:encoded:
Oh my goodness, thank you so much.

This might have been the first SCP I ever read (could have been the DVD of the Cavaliers game where everyone is trapped on the DVD?) and if not, it was the second, and it might scare me even more now than it did then. I don't understand why it makes me feel like I might shit my pants, but something about it… Poor Reagan.

Anyway, Voct, your explanation has helped me see it in a much less pants-shittingly scary way, and I can't thank you enough. :-)

item.title: Re: Explanation
item.content:encoded: That's quite the interpretation, I like it. I like the bit at the end "not attending Barbara Bush's funeral". I mean it's true, how could he have NOT gone, but at the same time, how COULD he have gone. It makes your mind wander. Good job.

item.title: Explanation
item.content:encoded:
It's a rehearsal.

The hooded figure is practicing for an attack on the real Reagan (who it really hates). It sets up an emulation of Reagan (who it really hates) inside the video, and has him do his typical STUPID BULLSHIT GARBAGE speeches (the kind that it really hates).

As the emulation of Reagan (who it really hates) rambles and babbles about stuff drawn from the memory of the hooded figure (who really hates Reagan), it slices and chops and mangles and mutilates him (because it really hates him). Sometimes it's so consumed by its hatred for Reagan that it forgets to activate its invisibility (because obviously you'd need invisibility if you were going to attack the hated Ronald Reagan in a public place).

Eventually, the hooded figure (who really hates Reagan) realized that the Foundation was watching it. This made it kind of nervous: the reason it's rehearsing so much is that, despite its vast and all-consuming hatred of Reagan… it has stage fright.

It wanted to say: "hey, SCP Foundation, I see you watching me. I understand that this is the kind of weird freaky anomalous thing that you guys watch for a living, really I do, but… honestly, I'm just practicing for when I attack Reagan. I'm just in the video, I'm not affecting anything external, right? So could you please, please back off? You're making me really, really, really uncomfortable, especially when I forget to turn on the invisibility and then you're all looking at me… honestly, you know he deserves it, you know he does!", and then it was going to run this extensive documentary it made that explains all of Reagan's horrible vile hateful sins from the moment he first got involved in Barry Goldwater's campaign in 1964…

… and it got as far as "I see you", and then it choked. Stood there paralyzed in terror for seven seconds, then burst into tears under its hood, and ran away.

So now, the hooded figure (who really hates Reagan) always checks if the Foundation is watching before it goes onstage. And it always remembers to activate its invisibility.

(It, uh… hasn't quite realized that Reagan's already dead. Oh, it listens to the news sometimes, both mundane and anomalous, and details permeate into its memory, but it never noticed the part where Reagan died. It thinks he's just festering away in retirement.

Most recent on its list of Reagan's sins: not attending Barbara Bush's funeral.)

item.title: Re: this day feels fitting
item.content:encoded: Hey, stay a while. The community's really starting to boom!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Y'know, I've been lurking since series II started up, and yet I never actually sat down and read through this until tonight. Wowie zowie, no wonder this is regarded as a classic. Good read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have really enjoyed this, and this is genuinely lovely

I hope the writer knows that there are some people in Korea who have really enjoyed this.

item.title: this day feels fitting
item.content:encoded:
Today feels rather fitting. To be honest, in my opinion, this is the scariest SCP on the entire database. By far. It's also my favorite, and a good example of what I feel isn't really produced by the Foundation anymore. Although I like lengthy SCPs (more space gives one more time to develop a narrative) I rather like this one because of its brevity. There is no narrative, there's only fear. It immediately pulls you in with a fast description, and the logs at the end aren't incredibly long, and can be read through quickly. The ending has a hell of a twist, and that black-hooded figure still haunts my dreams, the only SCP (or sub-SCP) to do so since 087.

I haven't been on this website for a while, so I'd thought I'd stop by. I thought I'd see what I remembered.

This was the first skip to actually make me scared for a few weeks after reading it. The last image is also one of the only jumpscares that I actually like.

Here's to 700 more upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those skips that comes around every thousand years and completely changes what it means to be a skip. The pictures, the narrative, and how it just gives me the creeps.

+1

item.title: Re: Seven. Twelve. Eighteen.
item.content:encoded: Holy shit what….

item.title: My Hypothesis
item.content:encoded:
I think video-Reagan is trying to warn us of future disasters, but he's not very good at human communication. Meanwhile, 1981-1 is trying to shut him up, but he's not very good at it either and keeps cutting him in places that allow him to keep talking. Finally, 1981-1 changes tactics and tries to scare us into not watching the video anymore.

Anyway, this is one of my top 5 SCPs of all time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was so refreshing to re-read this. I like these SCPS, that broadcast repeatedly with multiple outcomes. It keeps the possibilities interesting, and very open ended for future additions. On top of this, it's just simply well-written.

I'm thoroughly enjoying my re-visit to the site so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy
+1

item.title: Re: Harming A Former President
item.content:encoded: I'm no law expert, but not really, no. This is a creative work and technically falls under the first amendment. I'm also pretty sure there isn't any laws about harming a former president, outside of actively threatening to do so. If that was a law, all the movies depicting JFK's or Lincoln's assassination would be illegal.

item.title: Harming A Former President
item.content:encoded: Are you sure this is not against the law because a past president was harmed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
0:19:59 - Reagan: Further consensus has proven that over half of all Americans still hate. Eaten whole by void. The emptiness. The sadness. The blackness. The darkness. <laughter>

<laughter>

+1

item.title: Seven. Twelve. Eighteen.
item.content:encoded:
It's not 1981 after some random maths operations.

2007-12-18
December 18, 2007

United States District Judge Henry Kennedy orders a hearing on whether the Bush administration violated a court order by destroying CIA interrogation videos of two Al-Qaeda suspects. (AP via MSNBC)

Note that Reagan mentions Al-Qaeda after the sequence. ISO-8601, mates!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I said in chat.

There are no reasonable parallels here.

Trump's speeches do not show signs of anomalous behaviour.

Trump is not being sliced up whilst speaking.

The Black Robed Figure ™ does not appear.

What Trump says is what he says, rather than being a recording which says something different to the actual text of his speeches.

None of the speeches show signs of including comments along the lines of "Further consensus has proven that over half of all Americans still hate. Eaten whole by void. The emptiness. The sadness. The blackness. The darkness. " or "The decaying flesh of false morality poisoning our children. I have stood atop the mountain of this greedy earth, looking upon our beautiful pious pit, filled to bursting with the vast hands of helplessness. And did you know what I saw?"
He may not be entirely coherent sometimes, and he may say strange things, but nothing resembles this skip in any way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heres my question. If we're pretending that the Foundation exists in a world superficially similar to ours, or rather, exists in our world but behind a facade wherein they hide their and the skips existence from us, how could they possibly not think that whatever this thing is, its currently active? Like, how would they hear repeated screamings of American Carnage, talks of cities of chaos and blood, allusions to horrible events that never happened, inane rambling and incoherent falsehoods, attacking the concept of the truth and not see the parralels and think this thing is doing something horrible? What reasonable person who lives in a world where this is real would not draw that conclusion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All about plausible deniability. If you call David Cameron a werewolf and an affront to God, you can get the site in trouble. If you can believably say "This is fiction and does not necessarily reflect my opinion or the site's," you're good and can write about Margaret Thatcher: Vampire Pegger all you like.

Edit:

  1. I am not a legal expert nor a member of staff so my opinion is not Officially SanctionedTM.
  2. I think the site's done an ok job on not causing a libel (that's the word, iddinnit?) suit, including in regards to this piece.
  3. Margaret Thatcher: Vampire Pegger is my OC do not steal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't there a rule against political works aside from just the use of political figures like in this or the Bernie one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lmao if only

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: eh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really wish more SCPs dripped with the authors' anarchist leanings, and that writers flouted their black flags brazenly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You might be reading too much into it, my dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A glaringly obvious political commentary dripping with the author's anarchist leanings. Sharp and incisive, but Digiwizzard flouts his black flag much too brazenly for me to enjoy this as an SCP. Standing alone, this is an intriguing piece of art; on here, I must downvote.

I dearly wish he was around to discuss it - though I am sure at this point, he is celebrating Antifaschistische somewhere in northern Europe right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: GOD FUCKING CHRIST! Well, I didn't want to sleep tonight anyway. Along with the guy who erased his wife from existence, the guy who travels trough dimensions and the special personnel requirements, this is one of my favorite SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's so creepy, but them you get the stinger and you start to think about all the previous stuff mentioned……

item.title: Font?
item.content:encoded:
What's the specific font from the first frame (title)? I've seen it (or something meant to look like it) around but I don't know what to search for for that exact one.

(of course, this SCP is awesome but you knew that)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is truly a masterpiece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i dont know why but this scp has a creepypasta feeling in it

item.title: Re: 1000!
item.content:encoded: Don't be such a buzzkill when it reached +1000! :P

item.title: Re: 1000!
item.content:encoded:
Technically, it is not +1000. We don't count votes from deleted accounts, although the rating module still regards them.

And as for your comment, to each their own. High ratings are mathematically a matter of more people liking than disliking it. Besides, given that this SCP is well-known outside the site, popularity is way higher than the majority of SCPs made.
Finally, his reached 1000, although I don't get why is it so high rated, I don't think it is that interesting. Just my opinion.

item.title: Nearly 1000!
item.content:encoded:
Almost there. We can do this people.
BTW: Loved it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the single scariest thing I have read on here.

I
SEE
YOU

*shudder*

+1

item.content:encoded:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to say, this article became so much creepier lately because I found out that the Reagans were apparently very superstitious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's late at night and I need to go to bed, thanks for the nightmares buddy! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty sure it was made by the author, they also made me some sick images for Remembrance

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is that horrifying picture made? Did the author make it? That is some seriously impressive photo manipulation.
